config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu didahulukan tanpa kontrol, mudah sekali dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan atas nama mayoritas. Saya memang melihat pentingnya kepentingan bersama, terutama dalam hal keamanan, kesehatan publik, dan ketertiban, tetapi itu tidak boleh menghapus martabat dan kebebasan dasar seseorang. Di Indonesia, saya rasa masalahnya sering justru muncul saat suara individu dianggap mengganggu, padahal kritik dari individu sering diperlukan agar masyarakat tidak salah arah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter, but they should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why many people emphasize social stability, public order, and collective responsibility, because these values can help a large society function smoothly and avoid chaos. But I do not think that is a good excuse to ignore individual rights, because once the state or the majority can override them too easily, abuse becomes very hard to stop. A healthy society should protect the common good while still setting clear limits that defend basic personal dignity, freedom, and legal rights.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, saya justru curiga kalau frasa “demi masyarakat” sering dipakai untuk menekan kritik, membungkam minoritas, atau memaksa orang ikut arus mayoritas tanpa ruang membela diri. Kepentingan bersama memang penting untuk kesehatan publik, ketertiban, dan solidaritas, tetapi itu harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang tidak boleh dikorbankan begitu saja. Kalau hak individu dianggap sekunder terus-menerus, yang terjadi bukan masyarakat yang sehat, melainkan kekuasaan yang terlalu mudah menyalahgunakan nama masyarakat.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically outrank individual rights, because in practice that rule is too easy to abuse. From my perspective as someone from China, I do understand why people value stability, order, and collective responsibility, since a large society does need coordination and shared discipline. But I do not accept the idea that “for society” is a sufficient reason to silence criticism, weaken due process, or treat personal dignity as secondary. A strong society is not one that simply suppresses the individual; it is one that can protect the common good while still setting firm limits on power.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan tekanan terhadap minoritas atau membungkam kritik. Saya paham pentingnya ketertiban, solidaritas, dan kesehatan publik, tetapi semua itu harus berjalan bersama batas yang jelas: hak dasar, proses yang adil, dan perlindungan martabat manusia. Kalau hak individu selalu dianggap nomor dua, yang dilindungi bukan masyarakat, melainkan kekuasaan mayoritas atau negara. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru yang berani menjaga kepentingan bersama tanpa mengorbankan kebebasan dasar warganya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should come first in some limited situations, but they should not be allowed to override individual rights by default. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand the appeal of stability, order, and collective responsibility, because a large society does need coordination and people do care about the common good. But I do not accept the idea that “for society” is enough to justify silencing criticism, ignoring due process, or treating personal dignity as disposable. In my view, a society is strongest when it can protect public interests without giving power a blank check over the individual.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
